workflows.name: Run tests
notes: Runs Selenium tests on a remote DEV server via VPN and SSH.
Stores the results in XLSX and a short .env file on the server, which it then downloads as an artifact.
Uses the `concurrency` group tests-${repo} to prevent parallel runs.

workflows.name: Pre-commit
workflows.trigger: push to dev, PR to dev/test, workflow_dispatch
notes: Installs Python 3.12 and the dependencies from `webclient/requirements.txt` plus `pip-licenses` and `pre-commit`.
Before the pre-commit run itself it may (depending on the inputs) generate the license document `python_knihovny.rst`.
The pre-commit log is parsed for docstring and flake8 warnings; the output is published to the GitHub Summary and as a sticky comment on the PR.
If pre-commit generates changes, an automatic PR `pre-commit-fixes/...` is created using `peter-evans/create-pull-request`.

workflows.name: Deployment to selected environment
workflows.trigger: workflow_dispatch (tag + selection of DEV/TEST and the Docker image source)
notes: Via VPN and SSH, deploys the given tag to the DEV or TEST server using `scripts/ci_deployment/deploy_server.sh`.
Uses the `concurrency` group deploy-${repo}-${environment} to separate DEV/TEST runs.

workflows.name: Docker Scout
notes: Builds the Docker image from the Dockerfile and, using Docker Scout, compares vulnerabilities against the latest version in the registry.
Filters only critical and high vulnerabilities; comments the result on the PR.

workflows.name: Pre-commit autoupdate
notes: Runs `pre-commit autoupdate` on the test branch and, in case of changes, creates a PR `pre-commit-autoupdate`.
The log from autoupdate is inserted into the PR body.

workflows.name: Build and publish Docker images
notes: Builds and publishes the Docker image for prod/proxy/redis to GHCR and DockerHub, including cache, a cosign signature, SLSA provenance, and an SBOM (Trivy).
From the resulting SBOM and vulnerability scans it generates and uploads SARIF reports via `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif`.

workflows.name: New version
workflows.trigger: workflow_dispatch (new tag vX.Y.Z, optional CITATION.cff update, testing/production environment)
notes: First (job update_files) it updates `CITATION.cff` on the dev branch (if enabled) using `yq` and generates a commit.
Then it creates an annotated Git tag via `rickstaa/action-create-tag` and passes it to the `publish_images.yml` workflow for build/publish.
Uses a GitHub App token (AISCR AMCR Actions) for writing to the repository.
Uses the black profile; unifies imports across the project.
Formatting with a line-length of 120 characters.
Enforces PEP8 + project exceptions (see .flake8).
A non-blocking reminder about the docstring style for methods; runs `docs/check_method_docstrings.py`.
Always runs `docs/generate_module_docs.py`; the generated documentation is part of the pre-commit run.
Always runs `docs/generate_selenium_test_docs.py`; synchronizes the documentation of the Selenium tests.
Selenium tests run on a remote environment (see `scripts/start_selenium_tests.sh` and the docker-compose-test.yml mount `/opt/selenium_test`).
The test documentation (`selenium_testy.rst`) is generated automatically as part of the pre-commit hooks.

security_scanning.codeql: No separate CodeQL workflow is used; instead, Trivy + SARIF upload via `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif` in publish_images.yml is used.
security_scanning.note: Without a separate static code-scan workflow; security scanning is focused on the Docker image (Trivy, Docker Scout) and not on the Python code.

issues.description: The project relies on Docker Scout and Trivy SARIF for image scanning, but has no separate CodeQL workflow for static analysis of the Python code or a Dependabot configuration for dependency tracking.
issues.recommendation: Consider adding `.github/dependabot.yml` for Python and GitHub Actions and a simple CodeQL workflow focused on Python (at least on the dev branch).

issues.description: The `pre_commit.yml` workflow installs the full `webclient/requirements.txt` for every run, which can be time-consuming; however, given the more lenient 15-minute limit, this is not currently a problem.
issues.recommendation: If the runs start to time out, separate a minimal requirements set for pre-commit (lint/format) into a separate file.
